rationale: Ground truth's only substantive concern (dsocek's suggestion to derive naming/component matching from existing declarative data like _packed_modules_mapping rather than hardcoded lists) is echoed more sharply by X's finding that the manager should read pipeline._dit_modules (as the offloader already does) instead of maintaining a fourth hardcoded component list — a concretely actionable parallel. Y raises the same duplicated-list observation but only suggests adding a comment, which is far weaker action. Both show good grounding (independently corroborating registry.py:427's content and _dit_modules=['unet'] at line 46), and Y's transparent sweep methodology is a nice touch, but X's second finding (silent no-op for text-encoder LoRA, suggest a warning on zero-match) is also more tightly tied to the PR's stated bug pattern than Y's generic missing-round-trip-test suggestion.
